data.title: Trump Considers U.S. Withdrawal from NATO Amid Tensions
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed serious consideration of withdrawing the United States from NATO, according to a report by the British newspaper, The Daily Telegraph. According to BlockBeats, Trump criticized NATO as a 'paper tiger' and suggested that the U.S. exit from the alliance no longer requires further deliberation. This marks the strongest indication yet that the White House no longer views Europe as a reliable defense partner after allies declined to send warships to reopen the Strait of Hormuz at Trump's request.

When questioned about the possibility of reassessing the U.S. membership in the alliance after the conflict, Trump stated, 'I would say it no longer needs to be considered. I have never been convinced by NATO. I have always known they are a paper tiger, and by the way, Putin knows that too.' He further commented, 'We have always been automatically involved, including with Ukraine. Ukraine is not our problem. This is a test, and we are there supporting them, but they have not supported us.'

data.title: Global Media Revenue to Surpass $1 Trillion by 2030, Omdia Reports
data.description: According to Jin10, new data released by Omdia at the FED exhibition in Madrid indicates that global revenue from traditional television and online video is expected to exceed $1 trillion by 2030. This highlights a significant structural shift in the media and entertainment industry, with total revenue projected to grow from $775 billion in 2025 to $1.03 trillion in 2030. The primary driver of this growth is digital formats, particularly advertising. Online video advertising is anticipated to be the main growth engine, increasing from $309 billion in 2025 to $540 billion in 2030, with its share of total revenue rising from 40% to 53%. In the online advertising sector, social video platforms like Meta, TikTok, and YouTube will play a decisive role, generating approximately $400 billion in streaming ad revenue by 2030.

data.title: Expert Predicts GBP Weakening Against EUR Amid Rate Expectations
data.description: Francesco Pesole from ING has indicated that the British pound may weaken against the euro due to greater room for downward adjustments in the Bank of England's rate expectations compared to the European Central Bank. According to Jin10, Bank of England officials have been cautious in their language regarding interest rate hikes, and the bank was prepared to cut rates before the outbreak of the Iran war. Additionally, the UK economy is likely to be one of the most severely impacted by energy price shocks. ING believes the market will fully absorb the expectation that the Bank of England will not raise rates further. LSEG data shows that the UK money market currently prices in a cumulative rate hike of 48 basis points by December, while the European Central Bank's corresponding rate hike expectation stands at 62 basis points.

data.title: North Korean Hackers Launch Major Supply Chain Attack on U.S. Companies
data.description: North Korean hacker group UNC1069 has launched a significant supply chain attack on thousands of U.S. companies. According to Ming Pao, the attack occurred from the previous night until the early hours of Tuesday, March 31. The extent of the intrusion and the resulting damage are still under evaluation by experts, with initial estimates suggesting that recovery could take several months.

data.title: Gate TradFi's equity section has launched 50 CFD contract trading pairs, including AMGN and JBL, and is simultaneously holding a launch celebration.
data.description: According to the official announcement, the Gate TradFi stock section has listed AMGN (Amgen), JBL (Jabil), STX (Seagate Technology), STZ (Constellation Brands), INSM (Insmed), DOCU (DocuSign), QQQ (Invesco QQQ Trust Series 1 ETF), RF (Regional Financial), MPLX (MPLX LP), BUD (Anheuser-Busch InBev), SE (Wintersea Group), IBN (Industrial Credit Investment Bank), PSIX (Power Solutions), GRAB (Grab Holdings), REGN (Regeneron Pharmaceuticals), MSTR (MicroStrategy), GLXY (Galaxy Digital), UL (Unilever), NOW (Now Services), VZ (Verizon), LLY (Eli Lilly), WY (Weehau), MOH (Molina Healthcare), CMCSA (Comcast), RACE (Ferrari), ZIM (Zim Integrated Shipping Services), PVH (PVH Corporation), RIVN (Rivian), PLAY (Dave & Buster Entertainment), WDC (Western Digital), and SBET. SharpLink, TOYOTA, FTNT, HTHT, MRVL, BMNR (BitMine), QCOM, FDX, HSBC, NVS, RBLX (Roblox), CAH, FUTU, ADBE (Adobe), COST, SPGI, FIG (Figma), SNAP (Snap), WDAY (Workday), and DAVE (Dave) offer 50 CFD trading pairs with 4x fixed leverage. In addition, the Gate TradFi Stock Zone will launch a New Listing Extravaganza from 17:00 on April 1st to 17:00 on April 15th (UTC+8). During the event, users who register will receive 30 USDT and can share a 100,000 USDT prize pool by participating in trading of newly listed assets, with a maximum of 3,100 USDT per person.

data.title: Hyperliquid's March Revenue Declines by 10.6% Year-on-Year
data.description: Hyperliquid, a decentralized perpetual futures exchange, reported a revenue of $64.1 million for March, according to DeFiLlama data. This figure represents a 10.6% decrease compared to the same period last year. The decline highlights the challenges faced by the platform in maintaining its revenue growth amid changing market conditions.

data.title: Hong Kong to Host Major Spring Technology Exhibitions
data.description:
Hong Kong is set to host four major spring technology exhibitions this month, featuring the Hong Kong International Innovation and Technology Exhibition, co-organized with the Innovation, Technology and Industry Bureau. According to RTHK, the event will see participation from over 3,700 exhibitors from 28 countries and regions.

The Hong Kong Trade Development Council (HKTDC) highlighted that this year's focus includes a dedicated robotics zone, showcasing more than 100 robots across various applications such as commercial and industrial, health and lifestyle, and entertainment and social sectors. Notable exhibitors include four of the top five global humanoid robot manufacturers from last year, as well as companies from the "Hangzhou Six Dragons" and "Shenzhen Eight Kings" groups, alongside local startups and firms from the UK, Israel, and Singapore.

Additionally, the Government Digital Policy Office will once again set up the "Smart Hong Kong Pavilion," featuring over 100 innovative technology solutions. These solutions span more than 20 government departments and public institutions, as well as award-winning projects from the local innovation and technology community and students. This year's pavilion theme is "AI + Hong Kong," with eight exhibition zones focusing on the application of artificial intelligence in various sectors in Hong Kong, including "AI + Public Services" and "AI + Medical Innovation."

data.title: Ethereum Researcher Proposes Partial Stateless Solution to Address Network Growth
data.description:
Ethereum Foundation researcher CPerezz introduced the 'Verify Only Partial Stateless' (VOPS) solution at the EthCC[9] conference. According to Foresight News, CPerezz highlighted that the continuous expansion of Ethereum's state poses a long-term threat to decentralization by increasing the storage requirements for running full nodes. VOPS nodes only need to store four fields for each externally owned account (EOA): address, nonce, balance, and code flag, eliminating the need to store complete contract code and storage trees, thus reducing node storage requirements by 25 times.

The solution utilizes Verkle tree multi-proof or zkVM-generated SNARK proofs to verify state transitions and updates local account tables through 'State Diffs' without executing full block transactions. Additionally, VOPS supports low-hardware configuration nodes to maintain memory pools and connect to the FOCIL mechanism, ensuring ordinary users can participate in transaction inclusion. CPerezz believes VOPS represents an intermediate stage towards a fully stateless Ethereum, balancing node self-verification capabilities with low participation barriers.

data.title: MEZO will soon be available on Bitget PoolX, with a total airdrop of 1,500,000 MEZO.
data.description: Bitget PoolX will soon launch the Mezo (MEZO) project, airdropping a total of 1,500,000 MEZO. There are two BTC staking pools. In the regular pool, users can lock up to 30 BTC to unlock 600,000 MEZO. In the dynamic cap pool, the unlock limit is tiered based on the user's trading volume over the past 15 days, with a maximum lock-up of 50 BTC per user, unlocking 900,000 MEZO. The staking channel will be open from 22:00 on April 2nd to 22:00 on April 7th (UTC+8). Users with a positive net BTC deposit during the event will receive a 5% BTC interest rate bonus coupon after PoolX ends. Additionally, first-time PoolX participants who meet the net deposit requirements will receive a 10% BTC interest rate bonus coupon. Net deposits will be accepted from 22:00 on April 2nd to 22:00 on April 6th (UTC+8). For more details, please visit the Bitget official platform.

data.title: ING Economist Predicts Slowdown in South Korea's Semiconductor-Driven Growth
data.description: Economist Min Joo Kang from ING has forecasted a potential slowdown in South Korea's semiconductor-driven economic growth starting in the second quarter. According to Jin10, while chip manufacturers have not yet faced significant raw material shortages due to the Middle East conflict, inventories of key inputs may be depleted in the coming quarters. Kang noted that if supply disruptions persist, negative impacts could become apparent in the latter half of 2026. She added that even if the conflict ends within weeks, these disruptions may still suppress manufacturing activity and increase cost pressures. ING has revised South Korea's GDP growth forecasts for the second and third quarters downward and adjusted the 2026 GDP growth expectation from 2.2% to 2.0%.

data.title: BTC Leverage Position Opens with Significant Margin
data.description: On April 1, a notable BTC leverage position was observed. According to BlockBeats, Hyperinsight monitoring revealed that an address starting with 0x9bb initiated a new BTC long position with a 37x leverage. The average entry price was $68,522, and the position size reached $4.35 million. Currently, the position shows a slight unrealized profit of approximately $8,400, or 6%. A take-profit order has been set at $69,178.

data.title: ADNOC CEO: Rising Food and Fuel Prices in Europe Drive Inflation
data.description: According to Jin10, the CEO of Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) has highlighted the impact of rising food and fuel prices across Europe, which are contributing to increased inflation levels. The CEO noted that the economic shock is spreading westward, affecting various regions. This development underscores the broader economic challenges faced by European countries as they grapple with inflationary pressures.

data.title: South Korean Retail Investors Show Strong Interest in U.S. Crypto Stocks
data.description: South Korean retail investors have demonstrated significant interest in U.S. crypto-related stocks and ETFs, with net purchases amounting to $627.55 million from the beginning of the year through March 30. According to NS3.AI, this figure accounts for 9.7% of all net purchases of overseas stocks during the same period.

data.title: Baiyin Nonferrous Announces Board Secretary Resignation
data.description: Baiyin Nonferrous announced on April 1 that the company's board of directors has received a written resignation from Ma Zaisheng, the board secretary. According to Jin10, Ma Zaisheng has applied to resign from his position as board secretary due to work arrangements. Despite stepping down from this role, Ma will continue to serve as the company's deputy general manager.

data.title: Polymarket Sees Significant Shift in edgeX FDV Prediction
data.description: A notable fluctuation has occurred in the prediction market Polymarket regarding the future fully diluted valuation (FDV) of edgeX. According to ChainCatcher, the probability of the 'Yes' option in the sub-market predicting whether edgeX's FDV will exceed $700 million a day after its release has surged from 10.5% to 33.5% within an hour. This significant change highlights the impact of recent developments on market expectations.

data.title: Strategy Maintains Dividend Rate on STRC Preferred Stock
data.description: Strategy has decided to keep the dividend rate on its STRC perpetual preferred stock at 11.5%, marking the first month without an increase since the product's launch in July 2025. According to NS3.AI, the company attributed this decision to a monthly VWAP of $99.95, which kept the shares close to their $100 par value, allowing the rate to remain steady.

data.title: Whale Opens High-Leverage Short Position on Ethereum
data.description:
On April 1, a significant market move was detected by Hyperinsight, involving a large-scale trader known for shorting, referred to as 'Silver Ironhead Bears.' According to BlockBeats, this entity opened a 20x leveraged short position on Ethereum (ETH) at an average price of $2,136, with a holding size of 9,353 ETH, totaling approximately $19.92 million, and currently showing a floating profit of $60,000, or 6%.

In addition, the trader has set up sell orders in the price range of $2,180 to $2,300. If the ETH price reaches this range, they plan to incrementally add another $20 million in short positions at higher average prices to further pressure the market. The combined potential short position could approach $40 million.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts Netanyahu's Potential Resignation by Year-End
data.description: A prediction market on Polymarket is actively trading on the question of whether Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will step down before a specified date. According to ChainCatcher, the event has garnered significant attention, with the total pool size reaching $1.064 billion. The most popular prediction among market participants is that Netanyahu will resign by December 31, with an estimated probability of 40.5%.

data.title: Italy's February Unemployment Rate Steady at 5.3%
data.description: Italy's unemployment rate for February remained unchanged at 5.3%, meeting market expectations, according to Jin10. The previous month's figure was revised from 5.1% to 5.2%, indicating a slight adjustment in the labor market data. This stability in the unemployment rate suggests a steady economic environment in Italy during this period.

data.title: ECB's Dolenc: Policy Focuses on Long-Term Goals, Not Market Expectations
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council member Dolenc emphasized that the ECB's policy decisions are driven by long-term objectives rather than short-term market expectations. According to Jin10, Dolenc stated that achieving medium to long-term targets is the priority, and temporary deviations from these goals will not dictate policy changes. This approach underscores the ECB's commitment to maintaining stability and achieving sustainable economic growth over time.
